def: Of or pertaining to a prebend; holding a prebend; as, aprebendal priest or stall. Chesterfield.
Money paid by a tenant in lieu of the service of average.
Having three ports; specif.: Designating a type of two-cycleinternal-combustion engine in which the mixture enters the crank casethrough a port uncovered by the piston near the end of its stroke.
Bearing corymbs of flowers or fruit.
An order of brachiopods, in which the valves of shell are notarticulated by a hinge. It includes the Lingula, Discina, and alliedforms. [Written also Lyopoma.]
A greedy or ravenous eater; a glutton. See Gormand.That great gourmand, fat Apicius B. Jonson.
Serving to designate or indicate; pointing out.
Of or pertaining to pyæmia; of the nature of pyæmia.
Extreme depression or sudden failing o
The common soft tissue which unites the polyps of a compoundhydroid. See Hydroidea.
